items.summary_en: "Punggol" is a new district located in the northeast of Singapore. It has a cute Chinese name: "Punggol", which sounds like the sound of some kind of animal. Punggol is actually a Malay word. Translated, it means hitting a fruit tree with a wooden stick to knock down the fruit. In reality, Punggol Town has been covered by large tracts of forest for a long time. When the office towers of Singapore's Orchard Road were rising one after another, Punggol was still a wild land.
